title: Italian soft nougat
subtitle: Honey and sugar nougat with almonds and pistachios
excerpt: Torrone-style soft nougat made by whipping a hot honey-sugar syrup into beaten egg whites, then folding in toasted almonds and pistachios. The result is white, chewy, and intensely flavoured. Cut into 30 small pieces once set.
sourceNotes: Torrone (Italian nougat) is documented from at least the medieval period, with the Cremonese and Benevento forms codified in nineteenth-century Italian confectionery writing. Recipes appear in Artusi, La Scienza in Cucina e l'Arte di Mangiar Bene (1891, public domain), and in pre-1928 Italian confectionery manuals available through Italian national library digital archives. The whipped egg white and hot syrup method is consistent across all pre-1928 sources for soft-style Italian nougat.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps wrapped in greaseproof paper in an airtight tin for 2 weeks.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: Stovetop sugar work. Sugar syrup target: 145°C (hard ball stage). Honey target: 130°C. A sugar thermometer is required.

glossaryTerms.definition: A stage in sugar cooking reached at 121 to 130°C, where a small amount of syrup dropped into cold water forms a firm ball that holds its shape but can be deformed with pressure. For nougat, the sugar syrup is cooked to around 145°C and the honey to 130°C before being streamed into whipped egg whites. If the sugar is too cool, the nougat will not set; if it is too hot, it will set too hard and crack rather than chew. A sugar thermometer is not optional for this recipe.

content.text: Italian soft nougat requires a stand mixer, a sugar thermometer, and attentiveness: two sugar mixtures cooked to specific temperatures, streamed into whipped egg whites, then held at temperature while the nuts are folded in. The
of the syrups determines the final texture. Thirty pieces. Wrap individually in greaseproof paper once cut.
Cook the syrups and whip the whites

Have all your equipment ready before starting: syrups move fast once they reach temperature. Line the baking tin with baking paper. In one saucepan, heat the sugar, glucose, and water over medium-high heat until the thermometer reads 145°C; do not stir once it is boiling. In a separate saucepan, heat the honey to 130°C. At the same time, whip the egg whites with a pinch of salt in the stand mixer to stiff peaks.

With the mixer running on medium speed, pour the hot honey in a slow stream down the side of the bowl into the egg whites, then pour in the sugar syrup in the same way. Increase to high speed and beat for 8 to 10 minutes until the mixture is thick, white, and holds a ribbon. It will feel very stiff. Working quickly, fold in the toasted almonds, pistachios, and vanilla. Scrape into the lined tin, press flat with dampened hands, and leave to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours. Cut into pieces with an oiled knife.
